Commentary: Nathan Smith to Jayden Seales, no run, slanting into the pads from round the wicket, Seales brings his bat down and drops it to mid-wicket
Nathan Smith to Jayden Seales, 1 run, short and wide, Seales had all the time in the world to open the face of the bat and guide it to third man for a single

Nathan Smith to Shimron Hetmyer, 1 run, poor slower delivery from Smith! Way down the leg and Hetmyer glances it to short fine for an easy single. There's an overthrow as well but Hetmyer realises he won't be able to come back for two and won't retain the strike, so they decide against it
Jacob Duffy to Shimron Hetmyer, no run, full and close to off, Hetmyer clears his front leg and decides to go downtown, doesn't get much behind it as the ball goes to the fielder on one bounce
Jacob Duffy to Shimron Hetmyer, no run, swings and misses! The line is well outside off, rolling the fingers over and Hetmyer tries to heave without much feet movement. Misses!
Jacob Duffy to Shimron Hetmyer, SIX, shot! What a shot under pressure! Low full-toss close to off, Hetmyer brings the bottom hand into play and smokes it over deep mid-wicket for a maximum
Jacob Duffy to Shimron Hetmyer, wide, short and angling away from the batter, well outside the guideline. Wide given

Jacob Duffy to Shimron Hetmyer, SIX, he has sent that into orbit! Hetmyer, you beauty! Cutter around off, sits up nicely as Hetmyer muscles it over deep mid-wicket for 89m six. Into the roof, and we will need a new ball! Just 4 needed now!
Jacob Duffy to Shimron Hetmyer, no run, short and gripping away around off at 117kph, Hetmyer sways away and lets it go to the keeper
Jacob Duffy to Shimron Hetmyer, no run, comes round the wicket and hits the back of a length pace-on, Hetmyer tries to heave through the off-side but doesn't get any bat to it
Nathan Smith to Jayden Seales, no run, tailing into the pads from round the wicket, Seales tries to get some bat to it but fails. Hit on the pads as Smith goes quickly to collect the ball

Nathan Smith to Jayden Seales, no run, slower and shorter outside off at 115.4kph, Seales stands tall and pats it to the point region. He can play a maiden if he wants to, West Indies have all the time in the world

Nathan Smith to Jayden Seales, FOUR, not Shimron Hetmyer, it's Jayden Seales who finishes the game for West Indies! Why wait for Hetmyer to do the job when you are good enough to do it yourself. Half-volley from round the wicket and Seales lifts it over mid-on for a boundary. Not a great delivery, but Seales does well to not hit it hard and just time it over the inner circle

22:26 Local Time, 02:26 GMT, 07:56 IST: Shimron Hetmyer does to New Zealand what Mitchell Santner did to West Indies in the fourth ODI, but this time with a whole lot more Caribbean flair. The left-hander stays unbeaten on 69 off 64 as the hosts go over the line with two wickets to spare. West Indies end the series on a high with an impressive chase in the fifth ODI, powered by superb knocks from Sherfane Rutherford and Hetmyer. However, it's New Zealand who take the series 3-2. Remarkably, all five matches have been won by the team batting second. West Indies chased down 267 and 268 in the first and fifth ODIs respectively, but their struggles batting first proved costly. They were bowled out for under 150 twice, while their 188 in the fourth ODI wasn't quite enough as New Zealand edged home by one wicket. West Indies also dropped a crucial catch in the closing stages of that game, and had that opportunity been taken, the series scoreline could well have been different.

Talking about the match, fifties from Will Young, Nick Kelly and Tom Latham powered New Zealand to 268/9. In reply, West Indies openers Ackeem Auguste (34 off 24) and Justin Greaves (24 off 15) got the chase off to a flying start, adding 50 runs in under six overs, but both fell in successive overs. Keacy Carty and Shai Hope then steadied the innings with a 59-run stand, but neither could convert their start into a match-winning knock. It was eventually Rutherford (61 off 58) and Hetmyer who joined forces, adding a crucial 95 runs to put West Indies in a commanding position. However, Rutherford's needless dismissal, stumped attempting an ambitious shot, gave New Zealand a way back into the contest. Gudakesh Motie, Matthew Forde and Alzarri Joseph departed in quick succession, and it looked like West Indies were on the verge of throwing the game away. But Shimron Hetmyer held his nerve, stayed till the end, and made sure the hosts got over the line.

Mitchell Santner collects the trophy before the rest of the New Zealand players join him as they celebrate and pose in front of the cameras. That's it from this five-match ODI series. West Indies are next in action when they host Pakistan for a two-match Test series starting on July 25. As for New Zealand, they don't play again until October, when they welcome India for five T20Is, five ODIs and a two-Test series. That's all from us for now. Until next time, goodbye and cheers!
